items.title: Token-Flow Firewall: Semantic Runtime Auditing for Persistent AI Agents
items.abstract: Persistent AI agents extend large language models (LLMs) beyond single-turn interaction into long-lived software systems. Unlike traditional chat assistants, unsafe content in these agents can propagate through persistent state, reusable skills, and tool-mediated interactions, creating a substantially larger semantic attack surface. We observe that most security-critical interactions in such agents are transmitted through natural-language token flows, including memory updates, tool arguments, retrieved files, and inter-component communications. This observation enables a new security formulation: unsafe behavior can be intercepted as risky semantic flows before reaching privileged runtime sinks. Based on this insight, we propose TokenWall, a runtime defense framework that acts as a semantic firewall over agent token flows. TokenWall performs boundary-aware semantic auditing over these flows, constructing structured source-sink audit records, applying lightweight local inspection before execution, and selectively escalating ambiguous high-risk cases to stronger arbitration modules. Unlike prior approaches that rely on sparse auditing or remote large-model oversight, TokenWall enables full-coverage pre-execution mediation while reducing remote arbitration and latency. Experiments on CIK-Bench show that TokenWall reduces attack success rate to 12.5% while maintaining a 97.4% benign executable pass rate without human confirmation. TokenWall further introduces only 0.69 seconds of additional latency on benign cases, demonstrating that semantic runtime containment can achieve a practical security-utility trade-off for persistent AI agents.
items.llm_evidence_en: runtime auditing defense for persistent AI agents
items.llm_evidence_cn: 面向持久AI智能体的运行时审计防御
items.llm_evidence: 面向持久AI智能体的运行时审计防御
items.canonical_evidence: 面向持久AI智能体的运行时审计防御
items.llm_tldr_en: TokenWall proposes a runtime firewall that intercepts unsafe semantic flows in persistent AI agents, detecting jailbreak and other attacks before they reach critical sinks.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对持久AI智能体面临的新型语义安全风险，提出TokenWall运行时防火墙。通过监控令牌流（包括记忆更新、工具调用等）中的不安全语义，在到达高风险接收点前拦截攻击。实验表明该方法能有效防御越狱攻击等威胁，且对正常性能影响很小。
items.llm_tldr: 该论文针对持久AI智能体面临的新型语义安全风险，提出TokenWall运行时防火墙。通过监控令牌流（包括记忆更新、工具调用等）中的不安全语义，在到达高风险接收点前拦截攻击。实验表明该方法能有效防御越狱攻击等威胁，且对正常性能影响很小。
